title: Oxyspora montana
taxonomy: Oxyspora montana is classified in the family Melastomataceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the melastome or memecylid family. This family consists of around 150 genera and over 5,000 species. Oxyspora montana belongs to the tribe Melastomeae, which is a large and diverse group of plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The species is also classified in the genus Oxyspora, which contains around 30 species found primarily in Southeast Asia. Oxyspora montana is a species of flowering plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Southeast Asia.

intro: Oxyspora montana is a perennial herbaceous plant species belonging to the family Melastomataceae. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Oxyspora montana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
